Japanese: １０月１８日　某時刻 成歩堂法律事務所
English: October 18 Wright & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: ああ、つかれたなあ 今日は。
English: Whew, what a day.

Japanese: なるほどくん。 ダメだよくつろいじゃあ！
English: This is no time for relaxing, Nick!

Japanese: ねえねえ。 ニボサブさん、だいじょうぶ？
English: Say, you think WP's got a chance?

Japanese: とりあえず、今日の法廷で 話に出た人たちしだいかな。
English: I guess that really depends on the people we found out about in today's trial.

Japanese: カントクと、プロデューサー？
English: The director and producer...?

Japanese: そう。
English: Yeah, them.

Japanese: じゃあ、こんなところにいないで、 撮影所に行こうよお！
English: Well, what are we doing here then!? Let's get to the studios!

Japanese: 千尋さんのお気に入りだった 観葉植物だ。
English: Mia's favorite potted plant.

Japanese: 最近は、真宵ちゃんがさんざん 水をやるので、ふやけぎみだ。
English: Maya has been watering it so much lately it looks a little... swollen.

Japanese: かわいがっているつもりらしいが、 “水責め”に見えないこともない。
English: She's either trying to make it grow... or she's perfecting her water torture technique.

Japanese: 法律関係のムズカシイ本が イヤというほど並んでいる。
English: There's a horrendous amount of legal books here.

Japanese: これからは、ぼくが 読まなくちゃいけないのかな‥‥。
English: Scarier still is the thought that I have to read all of these now.

Japanese: 窓の外は、今日もいい天気だ。
English: It's a nice day outside.

Japanese: 最近、向かいの部屋は、 客の入らない夜はない。
English: The hotel across the way is always full these days.

Japanese: ボーイが楽しそうに 部屋をそうじしているのが見える。
English: There's the bellboy, happily cleaning the room.

Japanese: 千尋さんのデスクだ。
English: Mia's desk.

Japanese: 今は、ぼくが使わせて もらっている。
English: Actually... it's my desk now.

Japanese: ここに座ると、どんなときでも 気持ちが引きしまる。
English: Sitting here always makes me feel like a professional.

Japanese: 千尋さんが好きだった、 古い映画のポスターだ。
English: A poster of an old movie that Mia liked.

Japanese: 今度、ぼくも見てみようかと 思ったのだが、
English: I was thinking I should go see it myself.

Japanese: このポスター、タイトルが 書かれてないぞ‥‥。
English: Then I realized that the poster doesn't actually say the title of the movie.

Japanese: どうしたらいいと思う？ これから。
English: What do you think we should do?

Japanese: ど、どうするって、さっき なるほどくんが言ってたでしょ。
English: W-what do I... huh!? Why don't we do what you just said!?

Japanese: “カントク”と“プロデューサー” の情報を集めよう、って。
English: We gotta get the scoop on this director and producer.

Japanese: え、じゃあ、こんなところに いても、しょうがないよ。
English: Oh, right. Then there's no point hanging out here.

Japanese: だから！　はやく撮影所に 行こうよ！
English: That's what I've been saying! Let's get to those studios pronto!

Japanese: うーん。
English: Hmm.

Japanese: 何かを推理するには、 まだデータが足りないな。
English: We don't have enough information to make a case yet.

Japanese: 昨日もそんなこと言ってたぞ。
English: That's exactly the same thing you said yesterday!

Japanese: すなおに“何も思いつきません” て言えよ。
English: Just admit it: you don't have a clue what happened.

Japanese: データが足りないの！
English: There's not enough information!

Japanese: ほおら、これ。
English: Hey, see this?

Japanese: ‥‥ほんと、それ見せるの 好きだね、なるほどくん。
English: You... really like showing that off, don't you, Nick?

Japanese: あ。それ、まだ 持ってたんだ。
English: Hey, you still have that! Great!

Japanese: まあね。これがないと 現場のスタジオに入れないし。
English: Well, we couldn't get into the scene of the murder without it.

Japanese: 荷星さんは留置所だし、 いいだろ。
English: And its owner isn't missing it in his detention cell...

Japanese: うう‥‥。 かわいそうなニボサブさん。
English: Poor old WP...

Japanese: ‥‥やっぱり、これ、 荷星さんなのかなあ‥‥。
English: You know, what if this really was Mr. Powers?

Japanese: ううん。今のところ、 他に当てはまる人がいないしなあ。
English: Yeah. It's hard to say. We don't really know anyone else it could be.

Japanese: まさか、 オバチャンじゃないだろうし。
English: I seriously doubt it was that old windbag.

Japanese: じゃあ荷星さん、あたしたちに ウソを‥‥？
English: So Mr. Powers is... lying?

Japanese: それもなあ‥‥。 （こればっかりは、ナゾだ）
English: He really doesn't seem the type, does he. (Curiouser and curiouser...)

Japanese: あたしもほしいな。 トノサマン・スピアー。
English: I wish I had my own Samurai Spear!

Japanese: なるほどくんを 思うさまつついてみたい。
English: You'd be my own personal pincushion, Nick!

Japanese: な、なんでぼくなんだよ‥‥。
English: Me!? Why me!?

Japanese: ‥‥それがどうかした？ 何も思いつかないけど。
English: Sorry, I don't know how that will help us figure this out.

Japanese: １０月１８日　某時刻 成歩堂法律事務所
English: October 18 Wright & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: ‥‥とりあえず、何の手がかりも なくなっちゃったなあ‥‥。
English: Now what... We're fresh out of clues.

Japanese: カントクたちのアリバイはバッチリ で、他の手がかりもない。
English: Everyone's alibi is water- tight. We don't even have any promising leads.

Japanese: お先、マックラだな。
English: Things are looking pretty grim.

Japanese: ‥‥あきらめるのは早いわ。
English: It's a little early for giving up, don't you think?

Japanese: え？
English: Huh?

Japanese: まだ、手がかりは残ってるでしょ？
English: You've got one lead...

Japanese: ち、千尋さん！
English: M-Mia! It's you!

Japanese: ‥‥もっと 早く出てきてくださいよ！
English: What took you so long!?

Japanese: ごめんなさい。
English: Sorry, Phoenix.

Japanese: ピンチになって、真宵が本気に ならないと出てこられないの。
English: Maya has trouble calling me unless she's really in trouble.

Japanese: ‥‥ということは、 ピンチ‥‥ですか、今。
English: Right! Oh, wait. I guess that means we're really in trouble.

Japanese: ええ。かなり。
English: I'd say so.

Japanese: ‥‥あの、“手がかり”って なんですか？
English: What did you mean, we have one lead...?

Japanese: もちろん、あの少年よ。
English: The boy, of course.

Japanese: オレ、ゼンブ見てたんだ！
English: Yeah, I saw 'em. I saw everything!

Japanese: ええっ！
English: What!?

Japanese: でもなっ！　お前らなんかに、 教えてやるもんか！
English: But...but no way am I telling you losers!

Japanese: ‥‥あの子、協力 してくれそうもありませんよ。
English: I dunno. He didn't sound like he was going to help us at all.

Japanese: なんとか方法を 見つけるしかないわね。
English: I'm sure you can find some way of bringing him over to your side.

Japanese: とにかく。 もう一度、撮影所に行きましょう。
English: Either way, we should get back to the studios.

Japanese: もう、あの子が最後の手がかり なんだから‥‥。
English: That boy is our last hope, Phoenix.

Japanese: （あの子がねえ‥‥）
English: (Thanks, now I'm more worried than I was before...)

Japanese: あら。なんだか グッタリしてるじゃない。
English: My poor plant! It looks so... so sluggish!

Japanese: なるほどくん、ちゃんと お水、やってくれてる？
English: Have you been watering it properly, Phoenix?

Japanese: え、ええ。 真宵ちゃんが‥‥。
English: Um, well, Maya has actually...

Japanese: ギャクに、ちょっと やりすぎなのかも、水。
English: I think maybe she's giving it too much.

Japanese: もう。 ‥‥ごめんね、チャーリー。
English: That child! Sorry, Charley.

Japanese: （チャーリーっていうのか‥‥）
English: (The plant's name is... Charley?)

Japanese: 千尋さんが好きだった、 古い映画のポスターだ。
English: A poster of an old movie that Mia liked.

Japanese: あ、そうそう。
English: Oh, hey!

Japanese: 千尋さん。この映画のタイトル、 なんていうんですか？
English: Mia, what's the name of this movie?

Japanese: あ！　ひさしぶりに 私も見たいなあ。
English: That movie...? Oh! I'd sure like to see that one again!

Japanese: ‥‥あ、あれ。 なんだっけ、タイトル。
English: It's, um... What was it, again?

Japanese: ‥‥どわすれしちゃった。 ゴメンね。
English: S-sorry, it's right on the tip of my tongue...

Japanese: （うう‥‥ストレスが たまる‥‥）
English: (Let me guess... it's a suspense thriller?)

Japanese: あの、これから どうすれば‥‥？
English: So, what's the best thing to do from here?

Japanese: 撮影所へ行きましょう。 あの少年から話を聞くの。
English: We need to get to the studio and talk to that boy.

Japanese: 何か、気づいたこととか ありませんか？
English: So, do you have any ideas about who might have done this?

Japanese: そうね。何かを推理するには、 まだデータが足りないわ。
English: We don't have enough information to make a case yet.

Japanese: （‥‥なんか、どこかで 聞いたようなフレーズだな）
English: (Where have I heard that phrase before?)

Japanese: （真宵ちゃん、千尋さんのマネを してたのか‥‥）
English: (So THAT's where Maya got it from...)

Japanese: ‥‥私に何をつきつけてもムダよ。
English: Showing me that isn't going to do any good.

Japanese: ホラ、“死人に口なし”って 言うでしょ？
English: You know what they say, "Dead men don't tell tales."

Japanese: （しゃべりまくってるクセに‥‥）
English: (You sure are talking enough!)

Japanese: １０月１８日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: October 18 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: あ、成歩堂さん。 今日はありがとうございました。
English: Oh, Mr. Wright! Thank you for earlier.

Japanese: いやあ、運がよかっただけです。
English: Not at all. We got lucky, to tell the truth.

Japanese: ホントにね。
English: You can say that again!

Japanese: うるさいな。
English: Let's not.

Japanese: ねえ、ニボサブさん。
English: So, WP!

Japanese: 何か、手がかりになりそうな こと、ありませんか？
English: Do you have any leads that might get us going in the right direction?

Japanese: ううん‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 特に、ないですね。 ‥‥すみません。
English: S-sorry, but no.

Japanese: どんな小さなことでも 話してくださいね。
English: Be sure to tell us anything that comes to mind, no matter how small.

Japanese: 明日も運がいいとは かぎりませんからね。
English: We can't rely on getting lucky again tomorrow.
Y-yes... *sniff*

Japanese: ごていねいに、カメラで 見張っているようだ。
English: Smile for the camera...

Japanese: 面会のようすを監視する看守だ。
English: This guard monitors the visitor's room.

Japanese: 午前中の打ち合わせ、 カントクもいたんですか？
English: Was the director present at the morning run-through?

Japanese: は、はあ、まあ。
English: Y-yes, yes he was.

Japanese: トノサマンとアクダイカーンの 動きをつけていただきました。
English: He was directing how the Steel Samurai and the Evil Magistrate should move.

Japanese: なんで教えて くれなかったんですか！
English: Why didn't you tell us that before!?

Japanese: ま、まあその、撮影所の方から、 口止めをされてですね‥‥。
English: W-well, just, the studio asked us to keep quiet...

Japanese: 何、言ってるんですか！
English: That has nothing to do with this!

Japanese: 何度も言うけど、なるほどくん、 まだ新米弁護士なんです。
English: I hate to repeat myself, but Nick here is a newbie lawyer! Fresh off the bar!

Japanese: 負けるときは、ホント、アッと いう間にポックリいきますからね！
English: When he loses, he's going to lose big!

Japanese: は‥‥きょ、恐縮です。
English: I... I see. *sniff*

Japanese: （ポ‥‥ポックリはないだろう）
English: (Whose side is she on!?)

Japanese: 他に何か、かくしていることは ないでしょうね！
English: You aren't hiding anything else from us, are you!?

Japanese: は、はい‥‥ないと思います‥‥。
English: N-no, I sure hope not.

Japanese: スタジオには、ファンの子供は よく入って来るんですか？
English: Do, er, "fanboys" sneak into the studios often?

Japanese: そうでもないですよ。
English: No, not that I know of.

Japanese: あの警備員のオバチャン、 こうるさい人ですから。
English: That security lady's pretty strict with them.

Japanese: でも、今日の証言では、よく ウロウロしている、って‥‥。
English: But, in today's testimony, didn't she say they were often hanging around?

Japanese: 子供たちは、トノサマンが本当に 大好きなんです。
English: The kids really, really love the Steel Samurai.

Japanese: オバチャンの目をぬすんで、 入って来ちゃうんですよね‥‥。
English: They sneak past when she's not looking, I guess.

Japanese: 凶器のことなんですけど‥‥。
English: Regarding the murder weapon...

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes?

Japanese: 重いんですか？　あれ。
English: Is it heavy, that spear?

Japanese: けっこう重いですよ。
English: It's pretty hefty, yeah.

Japanese: ふつうの人は、自由にふり回すのは ムリだと思います‥‥。
English: I don't think the average person would be able to swing it around effectively.

Japanese: ふうん‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: なんか、なんか、午前中の 打ち合わせでコワれた、とか‥‥？
English: And... that's the spear that broke or something during the morning run-through?

Japanese: ええ。ボク、回しゲリを シッパイしちゃって‥‥。
English: Oh yes, I goofed on a round kick, you see...

Japanese: スピアーを、 折っちゃったんですよねぇ。
English: I broke the spear.

Japanese: とりあえず、オバチャンが なおしてくれましたけど。
English: The security lady fixed it for me right away though.

Japanese: ガムテープで、ね。
English: With duct tape. We heard.

Japanese: この写真ですけど‥‥。
English: Regarding this photograph...

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥。
English: Y-yes...?

Japanese: ほんとにコレ、荷星さんじゃ ないんですか？
English: This really, truly, absolutely isn't you, right?

Japanese: きょ、恐縮ですが‥‥。
English: R-right. *sniff*

Japanese: ボク、楽屋でヒルネを してましたからね‥‥ずっと。
English: I was sleeping in my dressing room the whole time, honest.

Japanese: でも、他には誰もいないじゃ ないですか！
English: But who else could have worn it!?

Japanese: あの、着ぐるみは楽屋に？
English: Your costume was in your dressing room?

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes.

Japanese: ボク、グッスリ寝てましたから、 持っていくことはできたかと‥‥。
English: I guess they snuck in and took it while I was asleep.

Japanese: （でも、いったい誰が‥‥？）
English: (But who!?)

Japanese: このカードキー、荷星さんの 楽屋から‥‥。
English: We found this cardkey in your dressing room.

Japanese: あ。ボクのですそれ。
English: Oh, that's mine, yes.

Japanese: このカード、部外者は 手に入れられるんですか？
English: Can people outside the studios get these cards?

Japanese: ムリだと思いますよ。
English: I don't think so.

Japanese: ボクも、この番組の撮影が 終わったら、
English: Even I have to return the card.

Japanese: そのカードは返さなくちゃ いけません。
English: Once the show's done filming, that is.

Japanese: ふーん‥‥。
English: Huh...

Japanese: す、すみません。
English: E-excuse me.

Japanese: せっかく、そのようなものを つきつけていただいても、
English: Thank you for showing me that, whatever it is.

Japanese: モウシワケありませんが、 心あたりがございません。
English: I wish I could think of some way I could help you...

Japanese: きょ、恐縮です。
English: S-sorry. *sniff*

Japanese: １０月１８日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: October 18 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: ‥‥荷星さん、取り調べか‥‥。
English: I guess Mr. Powers is in questioning...

Japanese: しかたないわね。
English: Oh well.

Japanese: ‥‥今日のところは、 話を聞くのはあきらめましょう。
English: Let's try again another day, Phoenix.

Japanese: 面会のようすを監視する看守だ。
English: This guard monitors the visitor's room.

Japanese: ヒマなのだろう。 鼻歌をうたっている。
English: He's humming. It must be pretty boring, standing in an empty room like that.

Japanese: １０月１８日　午後２時１６分 英都撮影所・正門前
English: October 18, 2:16 PM Global Studios Main Gate

Japanese: ‥‥誰もいないよ。
English: No one's here.

Japanese: オバチャン、 つかまっちゃったからね。
English: Right, now that they have Ms. Oldbag in custody.

Japanese: 誰か、かわりの人がいなくて いいのかな‥‥？
English: I guess they don't have anyone else to replace her...

Japanese: あっ、詰所の中に、 おばちゃんのようかんがあるよ！
English: H-hey! In the guard station! Look! She left her donuts!

Japanese: そ、そんな目で見なくても‥‥。
English: What? I wasn't going to eat them!

Japanese: この撮影所の正面入口だ。
English: The main entrance to the studios.

Japanese: 今日も、見張りの警官が 立っているはずだ。
English: They probably have a police officer on patrol out there.

Japanese: この撮影所のライトバンだ。
English: Looks like a studio van.

Japanese: 撮影用の資材などを 運んでいるのだろう。
English: They probably use it to carry around film equipment.

Japanese: 警備員たちの詰所だ。
English: The security guard station.

Japanese: オバチャンがむさぼるのだろう。 テーブルの上は、おかしの山だ。
English: Ms. Oldbag must like junk food. There's a mountain of it on the desk in there.

Japanese: この撮影所の見取り図が 描かれている。
English: An overhead map of the studios.

Japanese: ここから右へ行くと 《スタッフ作業場》
English: Right from here is the Employee Area.

Japanese: 左へ行くと《スタジオ方面》に 通じているらしい。
English: To the left are the actual film studios.

Japanese: 撮影所内のセキュリティカメラの 映像を管理するコンピュータだ。
English: The computer that runs the studio security cameras.

Japanese: ‥‥おや、今日は スイッチが入っていないみたいだ。
English: Huh? It looks like it's been turned off for the day.

Japanese: あ。‥‥どうも。
English: Oh... hello.

Japanese: あはは。やっぱり、そのカッコ じゃ、にあわないね！
English: Ho hoh! You look a little out of place.

Japanese: やっぱり、ダメですか。
English: It's the clothes, isn't it?

Japanese: ミリタリールックのベストを 着ているので、
English: I thought my camo vest might do the trick...

Japanese: “あるいは”と思ったんですけど。
English: Kind of an "alternative guard fashion" thing.

Japanese: 警備員たちの詰所だ。
English: The security guard station.

Japanese: セキュリティを管理する コンピュータが見える。
English: I can see the computer that runs the security cameras.

Japanese: あ。詰所の中のものには、 手を触れないでください。
English: Oh, please don't touch anything in there.

Japanese: 私、あのオバチャンに ハリ倒されてしまいますから。
English: The security lady would knock the stuffing out of me if she knew.

Japanese: 警備員たちの詰所だ。
English: The security guard station.

Japanese: セキュリティを管理する コンピュータが見える。
English: I can see the computer that runs the security cameras.

Japanese: あ。詰所の中のものには、 手を触れないでください。
English: Oh, please don't touch anything in there.

Japanese: 私、あのオバチャンに ハリ倒されてしまいますから。
English: The security lady would knock the stuffing out of me if she knew.

Japanese: （コテージのカギを 借りたいんだけど‥‥）
English: (I really want the key to that trailer...)

Japanese: （どうも、今はムリみたいだな）
English: (Maybe next time...)

Japanese: どう、撮影所の方は？
English: So, how are the studios doing?

Japanese: 警察の人がウロウロして、 大変ですよ。
English: There's police wandering around everywhere, it's terrible.

Japanese: “現場保存”とか言って、 そうじもさせてくれないんです。
English: They won't even let me clean up. Don't want me "disturbing evidence."

Japanese: おかげで、事件の日のお昼ご飯の 後かたづけもできなくて。
English: I haven't even cleaned up our lunch plates from the day of the murder!

Japanese: ああ。スタッフエリアにあった、 あのホネの乗った皿？
English: You mean those plates with the steak bones left over on them in the employee area?

Japanese: ええ。 メイワクな話ですよね。
English: Yeah. Can you believe it?

Japanese: ここには、よく子供たちが 入り込んだりするの？
English: Do kids sneak in here a lot?

Japanese: うーん。
English: Well...

Japanese: 数は多くないんですけどね。 よく見かける子はいますよ。
English: I don't think there's that many of them, but I do see one in particular a bunch.

Japanese: カメラなんか持って、 撮影風景をのぞいてるんですよ。
English: He's always gawking at the sets, or snapping pictures.

Japanese: 警備員のオバチャンも 目を光らせているんですけど、
English: You should see Old Windbag's eyes flash when she sees him.

Japanese: やっぱり、子供の行動力には ちょっと‥‥。
English: She has a bit of trouble catching him though...

Japanese: 今日、 裁判所で聞いたんだけど。
English: I heard something at the trial today.

Japanese: 事件当日、ここにはカントクや プロデューサーもいたって。
English: They said that the director and producer were here the day of the murder...

Japanese: すいません。
English: Oh, sorry...

Japanese: 私は大道具倉庫の方にいたので 見てはいないんですけど、
English: I was in the prop storage room, so I didn't see them.

Japanese: どうも、いたみたいですね。
English: I guess they were here, though.

Japanese: 撮影所長から、口止め されていたので‥‥。
English: The studio head seemed pretty eager to keep us quiet.

Japanese: やっぱり、カントクさんを かばったってワケ？
English: So they were trying to protect the director?

Japanese: カントク、っていうか、 プロデューサーの方かな。
English: More the producer, really.

Japanese: なんか、すごいヤリ手の プロデューサーなんですよ。
English: The producer's our real star here.

Japanese: ほとんどツブれかけてた この撮影所を立て直しちゃって。
English: She saved these studios from the brink of disaster and kept them running.

Japanese: この撮影所にとっては、カミサマ みたいなヒトらしいです。
English: I don't think we'd still be in business if it weren't for that producer!

Japanese: （‥‥プロデューサー、か）
English: (The producer, huh?)

Japanese: あの、これなんだけど‥‥。
English: Could you take a look at this?

Japanese: あ。荷星さん。
English: That's Mr. Powers.

Japanese: ‥‥でも、なんだか足を 引きずってるみたい。
English: Why is he dragging his leg like that?

Japanese: なんですか？　これ。
English: What is this, anyway?

Japanese: （この写真も、足のケガのコトも 知らないみたいだな‥‥）
English: (Looks like she didn't know about this photo or the injury that morning.)

Japanese: いや。 ‥‥わからなければいいや。
English: Uh... Never mind.

Japanese: へえ。弁護士さんも そのカードキーを？
English: Wow, they gave a lawyer one of those cardkeys?

Japanese: ま、まあ、借りものなんだけど。
English: Well, I'm sort of borrowing it.

Japanese: そうなんですか。
English: I see.

Japanese: なくさないように 気をつけた方がいいですよ。
English: You better be careful not to lose that.

Japanese: ここでは、そのキーの管理、 かなりキビしいですから。
English: They're very strict about those cardkeys here.

Japanese: （部外者がこのカードキーを 手に入れるのはムリみたいだな）
English: (I guess they're pretty good about keeping these out of outsider's hands.)

Japanese: うん。気をつけるよ。
English: Thanks, I'll be careful.

Japanese: 凶器のことだけど‥‥。
English: Say, you work with the props, right? About that murder weapon...

Japanese: ああ‥‥。 トノサマン・スピアーですか。
English: You mean the Samurai Spear?

Japanese: あれ、けっこう重いんですよ。
English: Yeah, it's a prop, but it's still pretty heavy.

Japanese: 私、あれを運ぶのキライです。
English: I hate having to carry that thing around.

Japanese: （あれを凶器としてあつかうには、 体力がいるみたいだな‥‥）
English: (So you'd have to be pretty strong to use that as a weapon, it sounds like.)

Japanese: （こう言っちゃなんだけど、 荷星さんだとピッタリだよな）
English: (Why is Mr. Powers the only person I can think of!?)

Japanese: ‥‥さあ。 すみません、なんのことか‥‥。
English: Umm... Not really sure what that is. Sorry.

Japanese: アッ！
English: Ah hah!

Japanese: アンタたち！ また来たのかいッ！
English: You again!

Japanese: きゃあ！　出たぁ！
English: Eeeek!

Japanese: 人をオバケみたいに 言うんじゃないョ！
English: How rude, acting like you've seen a ghost!

Japanese: ‥‥あの、ずいぶん早い お帰りでしたね‥‥？
English: You... certainly got back to your post quickly.

Japanese: あれからケーサツに つれていかれてサ。
English: Oh the police took me away, they did.

Japanese: 連中、トノサマンの着ぐるみの スペアを引っぱり出してきて。
English: They pulled out a spare Steel Samurai costume!

Japanese: “着ろ”って言うワケョ。 その着ぐるみを。
English: Told me to "put it on." Can you imagine?

Japanese: そんなもん、着られるワケ ないでしょうが！
English: How could I, a sweet little old lady, wear a giant suit like that?

Japanese: 荷星さん、１８０ｃｍは ありますからねー。
English: Mr. Powers is pretty tall...

Japanese: 着られないってわかったら、 帰っていいって。
English: As soon as they saw there was no way I could wear it, they let me go.

Japanese: （そりゃそうか。‥‥犯人じゃ なくなるからな‥‥）
English: (I guess that would rule out her being the murderer.)

Japanese: とにかく！
English: Anyway!

Japanese: オバチャン、このウラミは 一生わすれないかんね！
English: Know this, whippersnapper! This old lady NEVER forgets a slight or insult!

Japanese: もう、口もきかないんだから！
English: And you won't get any information out of me! My lips are sealed!

Japanese: さんざんしゃべってるクセに。
English: You sure are talking a lot for someone with sealed lips.

Japanese: 今からだよ！ ハイ、ヨーイ、スタート！
English: Starting now! One, two, three, mmmph!

Japanese: （コドモか、このオバチャン‥‥）
English: (This lady's too much...)

Japanese: 警備員たちの詰所だ。
English: The security guard station.

Japanese: セキュリティを管理する コンピュータが見える。
English: I can see the computer that runs the security cameras.

Japanese: 勝手にさわっちゃダメだョ！
English: Hey! No touching!

Japanese: （今は、中を調べるのは ムリだな‥‥）
English: (Guess I'd better investigate there later...)

Japanese: あの。オバチャンが見た 子供のことなんですけど‥‥。
English: Umm, about that kid you said you saw...

Japanese: 今度見かけたら、ハッたおす！
English: If I see him again, I'm taking him down!

Japanese: なるほどくん！ ホンキだよ！
English: N-Nick! Look at her eyes! She's serious!

Japanese: 事件当日、ここにいた カントクさんたちのことですけど。
English: About the director, the one who was here on the day of the murder...

Japanese: 今度見かけたら、ハッたおす！
English: If I see him again, I'm taking him down!

Japanese: なるほどくん！ 見さかいがなくなってるよ！
English: Nick! I think she's losing it!

Japanese: （今は、何を見せても ムダだな‥‥）
English: (I have a feeling she's not going to respond well to anything I show her now.)

Japanese: あれ。
English: Huh?

Japanese: オバチャンがいないよ。
English: Where's that old windbag?

Japanese: 不用心だな‥‥。
English: Odd of her to leave her post...

Japanese: （コラー！）
English: (H-hey!!!)

Japanese: あれ。‥‥オバチャンの声。
English: Nick! That was her!

Japanese: （待たんかコラー‥‥）
English: (S-stop! Whippersnapper!)

Japanese: ははぁ。どうやら、あの子供を 追いかけてるらしいな。
English: Y-yeah. Sounds like she's chasing after that boy.
Natch!

Japanese: あ、転んだ！
English: Uh oh, she tripped!

Japanese: （真宵ちゃん、 とてもうれしそうだ）
English: (Maya sure looks happy...)

Japanese: ねえねえ、なるほどくん。
English: Hey, hey, Nick.

Japanese: 今のうちに、ちょっと 調べてみようよ、詰所。
English: Now's our chance! Let's check out the guard station!

Japanese: そうだね。
English: Good idea.

Japanese: あっ。
English: Oh!

Japanese: これだよ、なるほどくん。
English: This is it, Nick!

Japanese: “コテージのカギ”って 書いてある！
English: The "Trailer Key"!

Japanese: ああ。あの第２スタジオに あったコテージの‥‥。
English: The key to that trailer in Studio Two!

Japanese: もちろん、借りるよね、 なるほどくん。
English: We'll be borrowing this, right, Nick?

Japanese: 《コテージのカギ》を ポケットにしのばせた。
English: Trailer Key slipped into your pocket.

Japanese: １０月１８日　某時刻 英都撮影所・正門前
English: October 18 Global Studios Main Gate

Japanese: ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ ‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ハア
English: Huff... puff... huff... puff ... huff... puff... huff...

Japanese: ‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ハア ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥
English: Huff... puff... huff... puff ... huff... puff... huff...

Japanese: ど、どうしたんですか？
English: W-what's wrong?

Japanese: ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ ‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥コシャクな！
English: Huff... puff... huff... puff ... whih... whippersnapper!

Japanese: （どうやら、今まであの子を 追い回していたらしいな‥‥）
English: (She's been chasing that boy this whole time.)

Japanese: ‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥今度会ったら ハア‥‥‥‥か、かならず‥‥ハア
English: Huff... puff... when... when I ca... catch him... I... huff

Japanese: （どうやら、つかまえられなかった らしいな‥‥）
English: (Sounds like she hasn't had any luck catching him.)

Japanese: ハア‥‥ハア‥‥こっちは‥‥ ハア‥‥ひ、人質をとったからネ！
English: Huff... puff... I... I got a hostage now, whippersnapper!

Japanese: （ヒ、ヒトジチって‥‥なんだ？）
English: (A "hostage"...!?)

Japanese: あの。 ‥‥あの子、どうなりました？
English: Umm... what happened to the boy?

Japanese: 今度見かけたら、ハア、ハア‥‥、 ヒネりつぶす！　ハア、ハア‥‥。
English: I-if I see him a-again, I'll... huff!
L-lay him flat! Huff... puff...

Japanese: ‥‥まあ。 ワイルドなマダムね。
English: My, what a violent old lady...

Japanese: さっき、カントクたちに 会ったんですけど‥‥。
English: Actually, I met with the director just now...

Japanese: ‥‥うう、シンゾウが イタい‥‥ハア、ハア。
English: Eh? Hah... my heart, it don't feel so good.

Japanese: （だ、だいじょうぶかな‥‥）
English: (Is she okay!?)

Japanese: ‥‥死ぬ前に一度‥‥ イブクロちゃんに、お参りを‥‥。
English: B-before I go, I'd like to v-visit the place where poor H-Hammer died...

Japanese: （そういえば、殺人現場に お参りしたいとか言ってたな）
English: (Right... she was saying she wanted to visit the studio where he died.)

Japanese: あの、“ヒトジチ”って なんです？
English: What's this about a "hostage"?

Japanese: ハア、ハア‥‥、 あの子、逃げているときに、
English: Huff... huff... W-when that boy was running away...

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥を、落として 行きやがったから、ハア、ハア、
English: ...he dropped this, a-and ran! Huff... huff...

Japanese: きっとコイツを、ハア、ハア、 ‥‥とりもどしに、くるはずョ！
English: H-he'll come back... huff... huff... for this one!

Japanese: （ううん。聞きとりにくい‥‥）
English: (It's kind of hard to understand her with all that huffing and puffing.)

Japanese: なるほどくん！ その“ヒトジチ”‥‥
English: Phoenix! That "hostage" might be what we need!

Japanese: 九太くんから話を聞くのに、 役に立つかもしれないわ。
English: Cody might talk to us if we gave him that!

Japanese: （なるほど‥‥） オバチャン！　その‥‥
English: (Good idea!) Ms. Oldbag! Might I...

Japanese: ダメだョ。
English: No!

Japanese: オバチャン、あの子を つかまえてやるんだから‥‥！
English: I'm catching that brat if it's the last thing I do!

Japanese: なるほどくん。何か取り引き できる材料はない？
English: Phoenix. Do you have anything you might trade with her?

Japanese: （取り引き‥‥ねえ‥‥）
English: (A trade, hmm...)

Japanese: （何かあるかな‥‥？）
English: (I wonder...?)

Japanese: ダレが見たってハア、ハア、 ‥‥ニボシだョ‥‥ハア、ハア。
English: I... I told you who I s-saw! Huff!
Huff... it... it was Powers!

Japanese: （まだ言ってる‥‥）
English: (Well, at least she's consistent...)

Japanese: オバチャン、これ、借りてます。
English: Ms. Oldbag? I'm borrowing this... just so you know.

Japanese: あとで‥‥ハア、ハア、 返しとくんだョ‥‥ハア、ハア。
English: Oh... o-okay... huff.
J-just be sure to... huff... give it b-back... puff.

Japanese: （つかれきっているから、 ちょっとタイドが弱くなってる）
English: (She's not as demanding when she's exhausted.)

Japanese: あ、それ‥‥ハア、ハア。 第１スタジオに入る‥‥？
English: H-hey... that... huff...
That's a card... huff... Studio One?

Japanese: ええ。カードキーです。
English: Right. A cardkey to Studio One.

Japanese: ハア、ハア。オバチャン、 イブクロちゃんにお参りを‥‥
English: Huff... puff... I... I could visit poor Hammer...

Japanese: お参りしたいんだよネ‥‥。 ‥‥ファン、だったから。
English: I'd like to visit... huff... he died... whew.
I was his... his fan.

Japanese: オバチャン、カード、 持ってないんですか？
English: You don't have your own card, Ms. Oldbag?

Japanese: あのへんは、担当じゃないから ‥‥ハア、ハア。
English: Studio One isn't my turf. Huff... huff...

Japanese: カード、貸しとくれよぉ‥‥。
English: You'll let me borrow... borrow it, then?

Japanese: （どうしよう。あのスタジオ、 入れなくなるぞ‥‥？）
English: (If I give her the cardkey, then I won't be able to get into Studio One...!)

Japanese: すみませんけど、ぼくたちも 調査しなくちゃいけないんで‥‥。
English: Sorry, we have our own investigating to do there.

Japanese: ハア、ハア‥‥。 ‥‥ハア、‥‥はあ。
English: Huff... huff... puff. Poo.

Japanese: （最後の“はあ”は、 タメ息みたい、だな‥‥）
English: (Was that last bit a breath, or a pout...?)

Japanese: なるほどくん。 ‥‥貸してあげたら？
English: Why don't you let her borrow it, Phoenix?

Japanese: そうですね。 ‥‥はい、じゃあこれ。
English: I guess it can't hurt. Here you go, Ms. Oldbag.

Japanese: 《カードキー》を オバチャンに貸した。
English: Cardkey lent to Ms. Oldbag.

Japanese: アンタ。
English: Listen to me, sonny.

Japanese: オバチャン、ヒトに借りは 作らないタチでね。
English: I don't like having debts to no whippersnappers.

Japanese: これ、持ってお行き。
English: You take this, and we're even. Deal?

Japanese: ‥‥なんですか、コレ。 トノサマンのカード‥‥ですか。
English: What's this...? A Steel Samurai trading card?

Japanese: あの子が落としていったんだョ。
English: That sneaky kid dropped it!

Japanese: アイツにとっちゃあ、 大事なモノなんじゃないかネ。
English: I figure it's pretty important to him, though.

Japanese: ありがとうございます。 ‥‥もらっておきます。
English: Thank you, I may have a use for this.

Japanese: （さっき言ってた“ヒトジチ” って、これのことか‥‥）
English: (This must be the "hostage" she was talking about.)

Japanese: 《トノサマンのカード》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Steel Samurai Card added to the Court Record.

Japanese: じゃ。 オバチャン、行くから。
English: Right. I'm off.

Japanese: （オバチャン、スタジオの方へ よろよろと走っていったぞ）
English: (There she goes, hobbling off toward the studio...)

Japanese: ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ ‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ハア
English: Huff... huff... puff... ... huff... puff... haaah!

Japanese: ‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ハア ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥ハア‥‥
English: ... Huff... huff... puff... huff... puff... huff... hooo!

Japanese: （‥‥答える気はないみたいだな）
English: (She's too busy catching her breath to tell me anything!)

Japanese: １０月１８日　某時刻 撮影所・スタッフエリア
English: October 18 Global Studios Employee Area

Japanese: あれ。あそこにいるの、 スタッフの子だよ。
English: Look, it's that assistant girl.

Japanese: おーい。
English: Hey!

Japanese: あ。あなたたち、 ニボサブさんの弁護士さん。
English: Hi... WP's lawyers, right?

Japanese: 聞きましたよ！ ダイカツヤクだったそうですね！
English: I heard about the trial! Great job, guys!

Japanese: いやあ、ははは。
English: Oh? Oh hoh hoh. Don't mention it.

Japanese: でも、警備員のオバチャン つかまっちゃったんですって？
English: Is it true they caught the security lady!?

Japanese: い、いやぁ、ははは‥‥。
English: Oh! Oh... oh hoh hoh!

Japanese: さっきオバチャンから 電話があったんです。
English: Actually, she just called me.

Japanese: とりあえず、あの排水口の アナをふさいでおけ、って。
English: She told me to cover up that drain...

Japanese: あ、ああ、あそこの。 （ものすごくブキヨウだな‥‥）
English: You mean that one?
(Wow, what a mess...)

Japanese: あ、あの、私‥‥。
English: I... I know...

Japanese: ちょっと、手作業はニガ手 なんですぅ‥‥。
English: I'm not so good with handiwork. Some assistant, right?

Japanese: えー。でも、大道具や小道具の 手入れとか、してるんでしょ？
English: Yeah, but you do work on the props, and the back- drops, right?

Japanese: は‥‥はい。
English: R-right.

Japanese: でも、手入れする前よりも ヒドくなったりすることが‥‥。
English: Just... lots of times they end up looking worse than they did before I fixed them.

Japanese: ま。ま。気にしない気にしない。 ‥‥そんなこともあるって！
English: Oh? Oh! Well, I'm sure these things happen. Nothing to worry yourself about!

Japanese: そうですよね！ 私、気にしません！
English: You're right! I won't!

Japanese: （気にした方がいいと 思うけど‥‥）
English: (Personally, I think she should worry at least a little bit more...)

Japanese: あ、あの。私これから、 オバチャンのかわりに、
English: Um, if you'll excuse me, I have to go to the guard station.

Japanese: 詰所に入らなくちゃ なりませんので、これで‥‥。
English: I'm supposed to fill in for Ms. Oldbag.

Japanese: そうなんだ。じゃあね！
English: Right! See you later, then!

Japanese: はい。 では、がんばってください！
English: Good luck with your investigation.

Japanese: “お茶”“みそ汁”“おしるこ” “くず湯”“おでん”‥‥
English: Let's see... cola, candy, chips, gum...

Japanese: ラインナップが 和風に統一されている。
English: Huh. It's so... normal.

Japanese: “ウーロン茶”すらないぞ。
English: I was expecting some cool theme snacks or something. Popcorn, at least!

Japanese: あ。なあに、この “トノサマン汁”って‥‥。
English: W-wait! Look! "Samurai Soda"!

Japanese: なるほどくん。飲んでみてよ。
English: Nick! Try this!

Japanese: 自分で飲めよ。
English: Be my guest.

Japanese: うん。‥‥あ、ザンネン。 売り切れだ。
English: Okay! Oh, too bad, it's sold out.

Japanese: 楽屋の入口に、 張り紙がしてある。
English: There's a sign on the dressing room door.

Japanese: “荷星　三郎様”か。 ‥‥さすが、主役だな。
English: "Mr. Will Powers"... it must be neat to be the star and have your own room like this.

Japanese: 楽屋に入るドアだ。 カギはかかっていないみたいだ。
English: The door to the dressing room. It's not locked.

Japanese: ねーねー、なるほどくん。 今日も、なんかもらっていこうよ。
English: Hey, Nick! Let's go in! Maybe there'll be something else to steal?

Japanese: ひ、人聞きが悪いな！
English: H-hey! We're not thieves!

Japanese: だって昨日は、カードキー もらったじゃない。
English: But we did take that cardkey...

Japanese: 借りたの、あれは！
English: W-we borrowed that! There's a difference.

Japanese: 楽屋とそとをむすぶ窓だ。
English: A window into the dressing room.

Japanese: くもりガラスなので、 部屋の中を見ることはできない。
English: The glass is frosted, so I can't see inside.

Japanese: 巨大な倉庫だ。
English: That must be the storage room.

Japanese: 撮影用の大道具が しまってあるようだ。
English: That's where they keep all the film equipment.

Japanese: すごーい、なるほどくん。 なんでも知ってるんだねー！
English: Wow, Nick! You know a lot about the industry!

Japanese: 見た感じを言ってみただけだよ。
English: Umm, what else would they keep in a film studio?

Japanese: モーターを乗せた、 電動自転車だ。
English: Looks like one of those electric-powered bicycles.

Japanese: なんともいえない中途ハンパな 感じが、ぼくはキライだ。
English: I dunno about these. I mean, what's the point?

Japanese: えー。でも、坂をのぼるの ラクだよ。
English: It makes it a lot easier to go up hills!

Japanese: 若いのに、あんなもの使うな！
English: Don't people cycle to get exercise!?

Japanese: 今日の裁判で言ってた 小学生の男の子って、
English: So, the fanboy they were talking about in the trial today...

Japanese: この排水口から 入って来たんだよね？
English: He came in through that drain?

Japanese: ‥‥らしいなあ。 大あわてでふさいだ、ってワケか。
English: So it seems. I guess they covered it up in a hurry.

Japanese: ね。ね。なるほどくん。
English: Hey... Hey, Nick!

Japanese: あのアナをふさがれちゃったら、 その子、もう入れないんだよね。
English: If that drain's covered, the boy won't be able to get in.

Japanese: まあ、そのために ふさいだんだからなあ。
English: Uh, yeah. I think that was the idea...?

Japanese: なんかその子、カワイソウだよね。
English: I feel kinda sorry for him, though. Don't you?

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ、また ぶっコワしておこうか？
English: What, you want to rip the grate off?

Japanese: え！　いいの！ なるほどくん！
English: Really, Nick? We can!?

Japanese: （‥‥げ。ホンキだ。 どうしよう‥‥）
English: (Whoa... she's serious? Uh oh...)

Japanese: やめておこうよ。 ‥‥せっかく修理したんだし、
English: Nah, I think we should leave it on. They only just repaired it.

Japanese: コワしちゃったら、あの子が オバチャンにしかられる。
English: And if we take it off, that assistant will get yelled at by you-know-who.

Japanese: ‥‥う、ううん‥‥。
English: Yeah... I guess so.

Japanese: （そこまでヘコむことか‥‥？）
English: (Wow! I didn't expect her to take it that hard...)

Japanese: ‥‥しかたないな。 じゃ、コワしていいよ。
English: Well, I guess some things are just made to be broken.

Japanese: わあ、なるほどくん、 いいヒト！
English: Yay! You know, Nick, you're pretty swell sometimes.

Japanese: （そうかなあ‥‥）
English: (Sometimes...?)

Japanese: えいっ！

Japanese: （なにが“ちょっと”だよ‥‥）
誰かが寝たあとが 残っている。
English: Looks like someone was sleeping here.

ROFL... LMAO!

Japanese: スタッフからの差し入れらしい おかしがならんでいる。
English: There's a bunch of snacks on the table. They must give these to the employees.

Japanese: あっ。なるほどくん！
English: H-hey! Nick!

Japanese: ど、どうした？

Japanese: （目に涙をためて、クチビルを ふるわせて、ぼくをニラんでる）

Japanese: ボクに“トノサマン”を作る チャンスをくれた、大恩人！

Japanese: 事件があった日は、いっしょに 会議をしていたんですよね。
English: You had a meeting with her on the day of the murder, right?

Japanese: 正午から４時まで、 ずっとイッショだったよ。
English: Yeah, we were together from noon to 4:00 PM, the whole time.

Japanese: あの、事件当日のこと、 聞かせてほしいんですけど。

Japanese: ああ。キミかぁ。オバチャンから、 ボクらのことを聞き出したの。
English: Oh, I know who j00 d00dz are. That security lady told j00 about us, eh?

English: It was a pretty regular day. We had a run-through for an action scene in the morning.

English: Anyway, I was in a meeting from noon till after 4:00.

Japanese: プロデューサーや、テレビ局の おエラいさんとさぁ。
English: With the producer, and some bigwigs from the network.

Japanese: （イブクロさんが死んだのは、 午後２時３０分）
English: (Mr. Hammer's time of death was estimated at 2:30 PM.)

Japanese: （今の話が本当なら、 アリバイ成立だな‥‥）
English: (If he's telling the truth, that meeting gives him an alibi...)

Japanese: “おエラいさん”というのは、 どういう人たちですか？
English: Who exactly are these "bigwigs" people keep mentioning?

Japanese: 制作局の部長やスポンサー、 それにプロダクションの人かな。
English: Oh, the boss over at the network, and some sponsors. Also a few production guys.

Japanese: 彼らはみんなでリムジンに乗って、 正午前にここへ来たんだ。
English: They piled into a limousine and got here right around noon.

Japanese: キンチョーしたよなぁ。（汗）

Japanese: その人たちとは、 ずっと一緒だったんですか？

Japanese: まあね。
English: Yeah, unfortunately.

Japanese: みんな白髪まじりの ジイさん連中だよ。（苦）
English: They're all gray-haired geezers... *scowl*

Japanese: （うーん、信用できる 証人たちみたいだな‥‥）
English: (Hmm... sounds like they'd all be reliable witnesses.)

Japanese: に、荷星だろ、これ。
English: T-this is WP, right?

Japanese: なんでわかるんですか？
English: How do you know?

Japanese: だって、ホラ。 足を引きずってるだろ。
English: Well, look, he's obviously dragging his foot.

Japanese: （そうか。このカントクは、 荷星さんのケガ、知ってるんだ）
English: (Oh, right. The director would know about the injury; he was there.)

Japanese: ああ、そうだ。プロデューサーから あずかったんですけど。
English: Here. I got this from the producer...
